narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a door slamming shut just as you're reaching for the handle. Our prediction model looked at your profile and estimated a 62.5% chance of not passing the bar exam down the line, which is why we had to make this tough call. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, placing you at the bottom of your class academically during that critical phase, and it was the biggest signal pulling toward this outcome. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 1.0, showing a pattern of struggling early on in a rigorous environment. Your LSAT score of 28 added to the picture, as it fell on the lower side compared to what's typically needed to thrive through law school and beyond. With your middle family income bracket, the model saw added hurdles in accessing the extra support or resources that often make a difference. Your Asian background and undergraduate GPA of 3.1 played supporting roles in this assessment, each nudging the prediction toward failure in their own way. Even being full-time and female factored in, though more subtly, as part of the overall story the model pieced together. It's not that any one thing defined you, but this combination painted a consistent image of challenges ahead on the path to the bar. That said, this isn't the end of your story—many have turned similar starting points into success by targeting those early academic patterns with focused improvement. You've got resilience in you; consider bolstering those grades and test prep to shift the odds next time. We're rooting for you to rewrite this chapter.
